name: admin-dashboard
description: Admin dashboard UI (SvelteKit + shadcn-svelte): login/signup/password-reset, a sidebar shell, and user + session management via the better-auth admin plugin. Requires auth.
env: # admin-dashboard: emails auto-promoted to the admin role on sign-up (comma-separated)

inject.text:
### admin-dashboard
- Admin console lives at `/admin/*` (users, sessions, organizations, audit, settings, account). Set `ADMIN_EMAILS`, then create or verify the initial account with `npm run admin:bootstrap -w <app>-api`; never commit or persist `ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD`. When sign-up approval is enabled, self-registered users stay pending until an administrator approves them on `/admin/users`; administrator-created users bypass the queue.
- Custom protected server layouts must call `requireBackendAvailable(locals)` from `$lib/server/guards` before redirecting anonymous users. This preserves sessions and returns 503 when authentication or maintenance policy cannot be checked.
- **Every list/table MUST use the shared `$lib/components/data-table.svelte` (`DataTable`)** — it gives sortable headers + a footer pagination. Add search/filters with `$lib/components/table-toolbar.svelte`. Never assemble `Table.Root`/`Table.Header`/`Table.Body` by hand.

instructions: Set ADMIN_EMAILS in .env (comma-separated). After building and migrating, inject ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_EMAIL and ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD for one command only, then run npm run admin:bootstrap -w <app>-api. The command creates a verified, approved admin or safely verifies an existing one.
Re-run the auth migration to add the admin and sign-up approval columns:  npx @better-auth/cli migrate -y --config apps/api/src/auth/auth.ts
Remove ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD from the environment after bootstrap. Keep ADMIN_EMAILS so matching future sign-ups are promoted consistently.

Admin pages: /admin/users (list, approve pending registrations, ban, set role, revoke sessions). Signed-in users manage their profile and security at /account; /admin/account remains available in the admin shell. Admin, account, authentication, maintenance, and API responses include X-Robots-Tag: noindex, nofollow by default.

Password reset emails go through the mailer module (installed automatically with auth); in dev without SMTP they are logged to the API console. Configure SMTP in admin settings or via SMTP_* env for production.
